subject: Physical Education
chapter: Changing Trends and Career in Physical Education

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The International Olympic Committee (IOC) has been instrumental in popularizing sports worldwide.
questions.reason: Reason (R): IOC has been the driving force behind the Olympic Movement, which has promoted sports development and physical education globally.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The IOC's efforts have indeed led to the global spread of sports and physical education. This is a well-known fact in the field of physical education.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The Indian Physical Education and Sports Sciences (IPES) conference is a major platform for Physical Education professionals.

opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: IPES conferences do indeed serve as a platform for Physical Education professionals to share their knowledge and collaborate on projects.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The Sports Authority of India (SAI) plays a crucial role in promoting sports development in the country.
questions.reason: Reason (R): SAI is responsible for setting up sports infrastructure, providing training and coaching facilities, and identifying and nurturing young talent.

(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: SAI's efforts in setting up sports infrastructure and providing training facilities have been instrumental in promoting sports development in the country.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Career opportunities in Physical Education are diverse and include roles in academia, coaching, and sports management.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The Sports Bill, 2011, aims to promote sports development and protect the rights of athletes.
